title: Docs Viewer Modal Consistency Delivery
summary: Establish the permanent Docs Viewer workbench pack, audit every management modal, converge lifecycle ownership, and correct styling and control drift.
content_html:
Docs Viewer Modal Consistency Delivery

Status And Outcome

In progress. DMC-P0, DMC-P1, and DMC-P1A are complete. DMC-P2 is the next approval-gated checkpoint; lifecycle implementation has not started.

Deliver one consistent modal system across the integrated Docs Viewer. Establish a permanent production-backed Docs Viewer pack in the federated UI Workbench, then use it to remove duplicated lifecycle rules and correct visible, tactile, and implementation drift between the shared ephemeral shell, persistent Metadata/Import/Settings modals, and Review Sessions.

This delivery proceeds only through explicitly approved checkpoints. DMC-P1A must simplify selection and comparison before DMC-P2 lifecycle work begins. It does not block Docs Viewer Core Refactor.

Start only after Docs Viewer Manage Theme Restoration closes. That delivery owns the initial manage-route and overlay dark-mode repair; this delivery owns modal lifecycle and component consistency against the restored theme baseline.

Benefits And Risks

The main benefit is one lifecycle and styling contract plus a cheap permanent inspection surface for current and future modals. This reduces accessibility regressions, duplicated controls, route-local correction work, and reliance on complete workflow setup for visual review.

The main risks are creating a second demo implementation, letting fixtures drift from production, or changing focus return, nested Escape handling, page scroll, action muscle memory, Import busy/cancel transitions, and long-content layout while removing duplication. Specimens must use production renderers and styles, and migration must preserve explicit workflow targets while proceeding from the simplest modal family to Import.

Accepted Contract

Shared Lifecycle

  • Every open modal has a labelled role="dialog" with aria-modal="true".
  • Each workflow supplies an explicit safe initial-focus target. Destructive confirmation does not gain primary focus merely for consistency.
  • Tab and Shift-Tab remain inside the active modal. Keyboard and pointer input produce the established focus treatment without leaving stale modality state.
  • Escape closes the active modal unless a workflow-owned nested control consumes it first. Metadata's parent picker continues to close before its enclosing modal.
  • Backdrop behavior and visible Cancel/Close actions invoke the same workflow cancellation path.
  • Opening locks background page scroll; closing restores the prior overflow state without moving the document.
  • Closing restores focus to the invoking control or the workflow's explicit current fallback.
  • Lifecycle listeners, scroll changes, status state, and temporary markup are cleaned up exactly once.

Presentation And Feedback

  • Compact, standard, document, import, and wide cards have deliberate widths, vertical placement, maximum height, and overflow ownership.
  • Headers, titles, metadata, fields, notes, status, lists, and action rows use one modal styling owner unless a workflow genuinely requires an additional local component.
  • Action placement is role-driven. Auxiliary and standalone destructive actions retain their declared order in a leading group; Cancel immediately precedes the single rightmost primary action, which may itself be destructive; terminal Close stands alone. Caller array order does not set layout.
  • Enabled, hover, focus, disabled, busy, and destructive states remain distinguishable in light and dark presentation.
  • Validation status uses the existing modal status region and clears when its owning field is edited or focus moves to another operation.
  • Busy feedback prevents duplicate submission and preserves the workflow's valid cancellation policy.
  • Terminal success and failure results remain visible until the user closes them.
  • Narrow screens stack fields and wrap actions without horizontal overflow, concealed controls, or competing scroll containers.

Ownership

  • The existing management modal shell remains the Docs Viewer rendering and lifecycle starting point.
  • A small lifecycle adapter may manage persistent pre-rendered markup; complex Import markup is not rewritten into generic HTML strings.
  • Metadata, Import, Settings, Review Sessions, scope lifecycle, document packages, source editing, media, and document actions retain their current domain owners.
  • No service endpoint, request payload, package selection, index selection, scope lifecycle, import, metadata, or review-session behavior changes.

Permanent UI Workbench

  • Admin owns the only visible Workbench route, exact specimen and named review-recipe selection, any required internal specimen-frame surface, and temporary audit-output reading.
  • App-neutral workbench support lives outside shared/frontend/; only behavior proven across real app packs belongs under shared/ui-workbench/.
  • Docs Viewer owns its specimen registry, fixture data, injected clients, adapters, and pack entrypoint under its test/workbench support. Admin loads that app-owned code; Docs Viewer does not expose a Workbench application route.
  • /docs/ui-workbench/ is retired in DMC-P1A. The Docs Viewer service does not own Workbench navigation, a standalone pack page, or serving workbench-only assets merely to prove a test surface can route them.
  • Serving the local inspection document through Admin does not transfer ownership of Docs Viewer production modules, styles, fixtures, or adapters to Admin.
  • Each specimen imports the real production renderer, component, and styles. No production markup, modal body, list, field, status, or action row is copied into a demo implementation.
  • The Docs Viewer pack exposes normal, empty, selected, validation, busy, success, failure, and long-content states where the production workflow supports them.
  • Family, state, and app remain useful registry metadata, search labels, and validation inputs. They do not form a cascading navigation interface; app selection is visible only when more than one pack is available.
  • The normal UI selects one specimen by exact searchable name. Theme and viewport remain explicit review conditions.
  • A new or materially changed modal records whether comparison is required. A comparison names its question, exact A and B specimens, review dimensions, and sequential or side-by-side mode; no comparison required is a valid decision.
  • Sequential review owns lifecycle, keyboard, state-transition, and narrow-interaction questions. Side-by-side review is optional and limited to presentation questions where simultaneous viewing reduces reliance on visual memory.
  • Named review recipes replace arbitrary pairing and broad related-control groups. The Workbench does not invite random comparison across every possible specimen combination.
  • The pack supports desktop and narrow viewport review plus light and dark presentation without writing source, calling mutation services, or requiring an external workspace.
  • A lightweight permanent check proves every registered specimen mounts without a browser error. It does not approve appearance or replace focused production behavior checks.
  • Selected browser-assisted screenshots are tracked centrally under admin-app/app/assets/ui-workbench/screenshots/<app>/, not embedded in documents or treated as exhaustive visual baselines.
  • var/admin/ui-workbench/ is reserved for untracked scratch captures, computed-style comparisons, measurements, exports, and generated review reports.
  • Studio specimens and Studio UI corrections remain a separate delivery.

The UI Workbench Guide owns the operational workflow and comparison practice.

Control Equivalence

  • Comparison begins with an explicit review question and exact named specimens, not a registry category or visual resemblance.
  • Selection cardinality, focus model, keyboard navigation, state semantics, loading/empty/error behavior, actions, and data ownership determine whether controls implement the same idea.
  • Same-contract controls should converge on one production primitive where the result is simpler.
  • Different-contract controls may share tokens or presentation while retaining separate behavior.
  • The exact specimen and implementation inventory remains code-owned.

DMC-P0 Fixed Contract

Docs Viewer Manage Theme Restoration is complete. Its semantic light/dark tokens and repaired manage-route/overlay contrast are the baseline; this delivery does not reopen foundational theming.

Construction And State Inventory

family production owner state contract
dynamic and ephemeral docs-viewer-management-modal-shell.js, called by document Actions, scope lifecycle, copy, package, source-editor, and media workflows ordinary field and choice input, validation, confirmation, result, and wide/long content; asynchronous work remains in the calling workflow rather than a second shell
persistent Metadata and Settings docs-viewer-management-shell-renderer.js plus docs-viewer-management-modals.js and the domain workflows Metadata fields, validation, parent suggestions, and long lists; Settings loading, empty, field, warning, save, and error states
persistent Import the persistent shell plus docs-viewer-management-import-controller.js and Import-owned renderers/controllers initialization, empty/selected file and collection states, preview/reconfirmation, validation/collision, busy/applying, success, partial, failure, and long content
Review Sessions docs-viewer-review-sessions-modal.js plus its controller loading, empty, populated, current selection, build/delete feedback, result, and error; no production caller currently composes this modal, so this delivery must migrate and inspect it without exposing a new Action

Control Semantics

control meaningful contract decision
text, boolean, native select, and radio fields one form-owned value with native focus and keyboard behavior share modal field presentation and validation rules; remain workflow-owned controls
Metadata parent picker editable single-value combobox with one active suggestion, arrow/Enter navigation, and nested Escape remain Metadata-owned; reuse Search List only if migration proves the exact combobox contract fits
Import file and package controls staged Import data, mode-specific single/multiple selection, counts, preview, and application states remain Import-owned; they are not the folder-browsing File Picker contract
Review Session rows one current session, row focus/activation, metadata, and separate Open/Build/Delete actions compare with Record List, but converge only if its selection, focus, and action contract fits without an adapter that hides differences
index checkboxes upstream document targeting owned by Index Multiple Selection not a modal control and not moved into Selectable List by this delivery
status and action rows common semantic roles and presentation, with messages and cancellation policy supplied by the workflow one modal presentation owner; do not centralize domain result shaping

Workbench Boundary

  • Admin serves /admin/ui-workbench/ and, only if isolation requires a separate document, an internal Admin-owned specimen-frame endpoint. No Workbench route is added to Docs Viewer.
  • Docs Viewer registry, fixtures, injected clients, adapters, and pack entrypoint live under docs-viewer/tests/workbench/. They import production shell renderers, modules, and styles; fixtures create data and callbacks but do not copy modal bodies.
  • Admin may load and narrowly serve the app-owned pack support needed for local inspection. This serving boundary does not move production or fixture ownership out of Docs Viewer and does not expose Docs mutation APIs.
  • Exact specimen or named recipe, theme, and viewport state belongs to the Admin-hosted Workbench. Family and state remain app registry metadata. Service data and mutation requests may not enter the pack.
  • Create shared/ui-workbench/ only if more than one real app pack later proves a shared contract. DMC-P1A removed the provisional cross-service protocol without an alias because Admin-hosted pack loading does not need it.
  • Reviewed captures remain under admin-app/app/assets/ui-workbench/screenshots/docs-viewer/; scratch captures and reports remain under var/admin/ui-workbench/.

Lifecycle And Presentation Boundary

  • One focused Docs Viewer lifecycle owner supplies labelled dialog setup, explicit initial focus, Tab trapping, keyboard/pointer modality, close requests, scroll locking, focus restoration, and exactly-once cleanup to dynamic and persistent renderers.
  • Escape, backdrop, Cancel, and Close enter the same workflow close policy. A Metadata parent popup consumes the first Escape and closes only itself. An Import collision or confirmation consumes Escape as its internal Cancel first; busy Import cannot be closed through Escape or backdrop when its visible cancellation policy is disabled.
  • Compact is for a simple confirmation, choice, or short field, including Settings; standard is for Metadata; document is for Review Sessions or another long body with one body scroller and stable actions; import extends document layout with Import-owned internals; wide is for previews and reports. All variants must fit desktop and approximately 390 × 844 in light and dark presentation.
  • Validation uses the modal's status region and clears on edit of its owning field or focus moving to another operation. Busy state prevents duplicate submission. Terminal results remain until Close.

Required Differences And Current Drift

Required workflow differences are explicit initial-focus and focus-return targets, Metadata's nested parent picker, Import's internal renderer and cancellation states, Review Sessions' current-row semantics, and the content needs represented by the five card variants.

The following are unintended drift to remove in later checkpoints:

  • the dynamic shell owns scroll lock and keyboard-modality cleanup while persistent and Review Sessions paths do not;
  • caller order currently places equivalent actions differently, and the compact modifier has no styling contract;
  • status attributes and empty/error presentation differ, Metadata validation can escape behind the overlay, and stale field status has no common clearing rule;
  • Escape in an open Metadata parent popup currently closes both the popup and modal;
  • persistent Import Escape can bypass disabled outer cancellation during busy or nested confirmation states;
  • Review Sessions duplicates document-level lifecycle handling and has no complete list/selection styling owner.

Delivery Checkpoints

DMC-P0 — Inventory And Fix The Contract

  • [x] Confirm Docs Viewer Manage Theme Restoration is closed and its light/dark token contract is the current baseline.
  • [x] Enumerate current modal construction paths and the normal, validation, busy, result, empty, and long-content states each can expose.
  • [x] Classify repeated lists, fields, statuses, and action rows by selection, focus, navigation, state, data-state, action, and ownership semantics.
  • [x] Confirm the UI Workbench host, app-pack, production-import, local-only serving, fixture, and temporary-output boundaries from the concept before changing code.
  • [x] Confirm the shared lifecycle, presentation variants, action order, status-clearing rule, nested-control behavior, and mobile/theme matrix before changing code.
  • [x] Confirm each difference is unintended drift rather than a workflow requirement.
  • [x] Keep the feature and this delivery concise; the code remains the exact inventory and contract.

DMC-P1 — Establish The Permanent Workbench And Docs Viewer Pack

  • [x] Add the smallest Admin-hosted workbench entrypoint needed for discovery and comparison without restoring the retired parallel UI Catalogue.
  • [x] Add app-neutral specimen support only where both the Admin host and Docs Viewer pack use one contract; keep production components in their current app or shared/frontend/ owners.
  • [x] Add a local-only Docs Viewer specimen page and registry that load the actual management modules and styles through current Docs Viewer asset ownership.
  • [x] Supply fixture data, injected clients, and callbacks that reach every agreed modal state without a real service mutation, source write, package workspace, or staged import.
  • [x] Provide the initial theme, viewport, registry-navigation, and related-control comparison controls needed to validate the host/pack foundation without adding workbench-only versions of production components.
  • [x] Add one focused boot check for registry validity, production-module loading, specimen mounting, and browser errors.
  • [x] Retain the central tracked Docs Viewer screenshot folder under admin-app/app/assets/ui-workbench/screenshots/docs-viewer/; keep scratch exports and generated reports under var/admin/ui-workbench/.

The P1 foundation used a provisional Docs Viewer pack page to prove production-backed rendering, isolation, and registry boot. DMC-P1A removed that page after the boundary was proven; it was not retained as a compatibility route.

The Docs Viewer registry contains 26 production-backed specimens across dynamic, Metadata, Settings, Import, and Review Sessions families. Fixtures and injected clients expose the agreed state matrix without mutation calls or external workspace data.

Guided use of the foundation exposed three assumptions that are not retained: cascading family/state navigation makes known specimens harder to reach, three broad comparison groups produce arbitrary pairings without a review question, and a standalone Docs Viewer Workbench route clutters the app that Admin was chosen to keep clean. DMC-P1A removes those provisional surfaces; their presence does not establish a compatibility contract.

DMC-P1A — Simplify Selection And Make Comparison Question-Led

  • [x] Replace cascading family/state/specimen controls with one exact-name searchable specimen selector; retain family, state, and app as registry metadata rather than navigation steps.
  • [x] Hide app selection while only one app pack is available and expose it only when the host has a real choice.
  • [x] Replace the generic compare checkbox, broad comparisonGroup matching, and arbitrary B selector with named review recipes.
  • [x] Give each recipe a stable id, review question, exact A and B specimen ids, review dimensions, and sequential or side-by-side mode.
  • [x] Keep single-specimen review as the default and make no comparison required an explicit valid decision for a new or changed modal.
  • [x] Use sequential review for lifecycle and interaction questions; offer the dual panel only for a named presentation recipe that benefits from simultaneous viewing.
  • [x] Remove /docs/ui-workbench/, its Docs Viewer service route, shell, workbench-only static mappings, and standalone controls.
  • [x] Load the Docs Viewer-owned pack entrypoint through Admin and use an internal Admin-owned frame document only where modal isolation requires it.
  • [x] Remove the cross-service pack URL override and message protocol if the Admin-hosted boundary no longer needs them; retain no compatibility route or alias.
  • [x] Update the Admin host, Docs Viewer pack, registry validation, focused smoke, concept, and guide together; remove superseded grouping controls and fields without aliases.
  • [x] Keep theme and viewport as explicit conditions and retain production imports, iframe isolation, direct fixture ownership, write-free operation, and browser-error detection.

Admin now serves the sole /admin/ui-workbench/ route and a generic same-origin specimen frame. Docs Viewer owns the pack entrypoint, 26 production-backed specimens, fixture data, and six named review recipes. The normal view opens one exact searchable specimen; four presentation recipes use fixed side-by-side A/B panels, while two lifecycle recipes use one frame with explicit sequential A/B steps. The retired Docs route, standalone pack shell, arbitrary comparison groups, pack URL override, and shared cross-service message protocol were removed without aliases.

The focused smoke opens the unmodified Admin route, mounts every specimen, exercises every recipe in its declared mode, checks dark and narrow projection, rejects network writes, and fails on browser errors. Focused Python contract checks and a live in-app review cover the serving and interaction boundary without an external Docs workspace. The registered Docs Viewer smoke profile passes all 17 checks.

DMC-P2 — Establish One Lifecycle Owner

  • [ ] Extract the reusable lifecycle behavior currently embedded in the ephemeral management modal shell without adding a second modal framework.
  • [ ] Keep the current dynamic frame renderer as a consumer of that lifecycle.
  • [ ] Cover initial focus, Tab wrapping, radio-group arrows, Escape, backdrop cancellation, scroll lock/restoration, focus return, keyboard-focus indication, and cleanup through focused module evidence.
  • [ ] Migrate Review Sessions first and remove its duplicate document-level lifecycle listener.
  • [ ] Preserve Review Sessions selection, action enablement, result calls, and invoking-control focus return.
  • [ ] Update the affected workbench specimens in the same change so old and new lifecycle behavior can be compared without a live workflow.

DMC-P3 — Migrate Persistent Management Modals

  • [ ] Move Metadata and Settings onto the shared lifecycle while retaining their existing field, save, warning, and focus targets.
  • [ ] Preserve Metadata parent-picker Escape precedence and navigation-row focus restoration.
  • [ ] Move Import only after the simpler persistent modals are stable.
  • [ ] Preserve Import initialization, file and collection states, busy cancellation policy, terminal Cancel-to-Close transition, internal scrolling, and manage-action focus return.
  • [ ] Remove superseded lifecycle callbacks, listeners, state, and selectors rather than keeping compatibility paths.
  • [ ] Keep the normal, validation, busy, terminal, and long-content specimens current through each migration.

DMC-P4 — Normalize Styling, Feedback, And Equivalent Controls

  • [ ] Define every supported card-size modifier, including a real compact variant, and remove meaningless modifiers.
  • [ ] Give document-style modal bodies and action areas clear scroll and footer ownership.
  • [ ] Standardize action order, wrapping, disabled/busy appearance, focus treatment, and status-state attributes across ordinary and Import buttons.
  • [ ] Add the missing Review Sessions list, row, selection, metadata, empty, and status styles through the modal stylesheet owner.
  • [ ] Use the DMC-P0 implementation matrix and workbench comparison to consolidate only controls with the same meaningful contract.
  • [ ] Reuse the current shared Record List, Selectable List, Search List, or File Picker only where its exact production contract fits; do not force modal-local lists into an unsuitable universal component.
  • [ ] Reuse current design tokens and remove duplicate modal-only rules where ownership becomes clear; do not consolidate unrelated Docs Viewer styling.
  • [ ] Apply the agreed stale-status clearing behavior without hiding terminal results.

DMC-P5 — Verify, Retain, And Close

  • [ ] Prove every registered Docs Viewer specimen still mounts through production modules and styles without network writes or browser errors.
  • [ ] Run the focused shared modal lifecycle module smoke and the smallest affected workflow/module checks.
  • [ ] Retain package, import, metadata, settings, scope lifecycle, and review-session request/result checks only where they protect durable behavior; do not duplicate ordinary click choreography.
  • [ ] Inspect every modal family through the workbench at desktop and approximately 390 × 844 in light and dark presentation with keyboard and pointer input.
  • [ ] Check initial focus, forward/reverse Tab wrapping, nested Escape, backdrop and Cancel, focus restoration, page-scroll restoration, action order, disabled/busy state, stale-status clearing, long content, and horizontal overflow.
  • [ ] Run one representative live integration path for each durable browser/service boundary rather than every visual state.
  • [ ] Confirm the workbench can be revisited after this delivery and that adding a materially changed Docs Viewer modal/control requires a specimen update.
  • [ ] Use browser-assisted screenshots where helpful, save selected reviewed captures centrally with filenames identifying specimen, state, theme, and viewport, and update or remove stale assets without embedding them in documents.
  • [ ] Update the smallest durable modal/runtime owner, run git diff --check, and close only when no current modal remains on a separate lifecycle contract.

Verification Boundary

Permanent automated evidence should protect:

  • workbench registry validity, production imports, and specimen boot;
  • shared modal lifecycle behavior;
  • workflow request/response and ready/busy agreement;
  • module wiring and management-only ownership;
  • explicit nested-control behavior where it affects modal closure.

Permanent workbench review should make these states directly reachable:

  • visual spacing and hierarchy;
  • button order and tactile feel;
  • focus timing and indication;
  • backdrop, scroll, and pointer feel;
  • light/dark presentation;
  • narrow-screen and long-content fit;
  • exact-specimen review and question-led named comparison where a delivery declares it useful.

Do not add exhaustive pixel-approved screenshot baselines, exhaustive markup snapshots, wording assertions, one permanent browser script per modal, or fixtures that duplicate production markup.

Explicitly Outside This Delivery

  • Changes to modal workflow purpose, domain validation, service endpoints, request or result schemas, writes, or action placement.
  • A generic cross-app modal framework, third-party dialog dependency, or compatibility layer.
  • Recreating the retired Admin UI Catalogue or maintaining parallel demo components, CSS, or documentation.
  • Public Docs Viewer behavior or assets.
  • A broad Docs Viewer redesign or consolidation of unrelated route, package, source-editor, or shared-component CSS.
  • New modals, new management capabilities, native-dialog conversion, animation, theming systems, or speculative action variants.
  • Index Multiple Selection consumer changes or partial Returned Packages behavior.
  • A Studio specimen pack, Studio UI audit, or Studio remediation; those require a separate delivery.
  • Foundational manage-route theme-token restoration or first-pass dark-mode repair; those belong to Docs Viewer Manage Theme Restoration.
  • Automatic calendar scheduling, release gating, pixel approval, or screenshot-diff infrastructure.

Completion Gates

  • every meaningful current Docs Viewer modal state is directly inspectable through a permanent production-backed workbench specimen;
  • known specimens are reachable by exact name without cascading taxonomy controls, and every comparison is a named question-led recipe rather than an arbitrary pairing;
  • Admin is the only Workbench route owner; Docs Viewer contributes app-owned pack code without exposing /docs/ui-workbench/ or another Workbench application route;
  • the Admin host, shared workbench support, Docs Viewer pack, production UI, and temporary-output boundaries remain explicit;
  • every current management modal uses one lifecycle owner;
  • every supported modal size and workflow-local component has intentional styling;
  • same-contract controls converge where doing so reduces duplication, while different-contract controls retain truthful behavior;
  • action, focus, Escape, backdrop, scroll, status, busy, and focus-return behavior is consistent except where a documented workflow rule requires a difference;
  • Import, Metadata, Settings, Review Sessions, scope lifecycle, packages, source editing, media, and action confirmations retain their domain behavior;
  • focused automated evidence and the complete tactile matrix pass;
  • obsolete lifecycle and styling duplication is removed without aliases;
  • the retained workbench imports production implementations and contains no parallel component library or permanent visual baseline.

Next Checkpoint

DMC-P2 awaits explicit approval. Establish one lifecycle owner and migrate Review Sessions first. Do not start Metadata, Settings, Import, or styling normalization until their later checkpoints are approved.
